title: fritillaria pyrenaica var. boissieri
intro: Fritillaria pyrenaica var. boissieri, also known as the Pyrenean fritillary, is a perennial plant species belonging to the lily family (Liliaceae). Traditionally used in folk medicine, it is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of saponins and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including colds, fever, and respiratory infections, as well as to soothe sore throats and coughs. Fritillaria pyrenaica var. boissieri is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or dried root, and is also used in traditional medicine to promote respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and asthma.
